sanskrit: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् । भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ  मेरेमें आविष्ट चित्तवाले उन भक्तोंका मैं मृत्युरूप संसारसमुद्रसे शीघ्र ही उद्धार करनेवाला बन जाता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I quickly become the one who lifts those whose minds are fixed on me from the ocean of mortal existence, O Partha.
essence: A mind fixed on Krishna is met by Krishna's saving presence.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Those who place their minds fully on Krishna are not left to struggle alone. He becomes the one who rescues them from the cycle of repeated death and return.
simpleInsight: Devotion is not abandonment; it is being held when your own strength runs out.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra field, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna is answering the devotee's deepest fear: being trapped in the endless ocean of mortality. He promises direct rescue to those whose minds are absorbed in him.
modernRelevance: You are sitting in the car after a breakup, staring at the steering wheel, unable to move. This verse speaks to the relief of letting a steadier presence carry what your grip cannot.
translationLiteral: तेषाम् (of those) / अहम् (I) / समुद्धर्ता (the rescuer) / मृत्यु-संसार-सागरात् (from the ocean of mortal existence) / भवामि (become) / नचिरात् (not after long) / पार्थ (O Partha) / मयि (in me) / आवेशित-चेतसाम् (of those whose minds are fixed)
